subject: [PATCH v1 12/17] fuse: add passthrough support for entry creation

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary: reviewer noted that we need to verify the type of the backing file matches the requested entry mode, citing lookup as a similar case where this was already enforced
reviews.raw_body:
I think we need to verify that the backing file is of the same type as
requested entry.

This was also true for lookup which instantiates the inode according to the
mode in fuse_attr.

Thanks,
Amir.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged the need to add checks for invalid node IDs and attributes in the fuse_process_entry2 function, agreed to include them in version 2 of the patch.
reviews.raw_body:
Great point, I'll add these checks in for v2.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-04-20.patch_summary: This patch adds support for passthrough operations in the FUSE file system, specifically for entry creation operations such as mknod, mkdir, symlink, and link. When FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO is enabled, it uses the new extended fuse_entry2_out reply to associate newly created inodes with backing files for passthrough operations. If a backing id is returned by the server, the inode is automatically associated with the backing file; otherwise, statx attributes are cached.
